calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: George Dierkes for and with (trustee) Edward Koplowitz.

The Court asked if there was anything additional to add and was told there was not. The Court stated that it was prepared to allow the accounting but not approve it. Mr. Dierkes stated that he thought that they had answered all of the examiner's questions. The Court stated that regarding the calculation of distribution it only received a general summary and non helpful responses. Mr. Dierkes stated that distribution was made pursuant to the court order. The Court stated that they do not say how the distributions were allocated. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-19-2015 Continued To Sep-29-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: George Dirkes for and with (trustee) Edward Koplowitz. Deborah Kerreos (beneficiary) in Propria Persona. Stacey Kerreos (beneficiary) also present.

The Court asked the beneficiaries if they were here to oppose/contest the petition. Ms. Deborah Kerreos stated that they are and that she had emailed Mr. Dirkes to say that she had not received documents. The Court stated that the matter will be continued to give time to file the objections, time for the trustee to review them and asked if Ms. Kerreos had notified the trustee of the substance of her objections. Ms. Kerreos stated that she corresponds via email but does not receive replies and that she would like additional time to obtain counsel. Mr. Koplowitz reported that they provided all of the information in the accounting in April, that this petition was filed in May and that she was previously represented by counsel. The accounting petition was continued to September 29, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The objections must be in writing and filed and served no later than September 18, 2015.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-26-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: George Dirkes for adn with Edward Kopolowutz (Trustee). William Ziegler and Douglas Applegate for and with Rita Schuman, for Andrew Schuman and for Stephanie Schuman. Stacey Kerreos, Debbie Kerreos and Petra Callahan all appeared in Propria Persona.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to allocation of the extraordinary Trustee fees paid to the predecessor, allocation of the mortgage on the Bay Street property, whether the Schuman family or the Kerrios family should be responsible for the payoff value on the loan that was taken out by Bessie and Howard, the equalization clause, whether the settlement agreement carved out said loan, the settlement agreement not being signed by the Trustee, who was on title, whether or not there should be any reimbursement of expenses, unknown taxes and attorney fees, date of death value, Howard's sanctions, etc. The Petition for Instructions was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Determination That Proposed Petition To Determine Heirship Due To Violation Of No Contest Clause By Howard Schuman And For Interpretation Of Trust Provisions Does Not Violate The Terms Of The No Contest Clause
Off Calendar May-14-2010 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jerome Marks for and with Stacey Kerreos and Petra Callahan. Douglas Applegate for Rita Schuman.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the law revision of the safe harbor petitions applying/not applying to this matter. Mr. Applegate stated that he had no objection to the Court taking judicial notice of the documents requested by Mr. Marks. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR#5520. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Bloom.

Petition For Determination That Proposed Petition To Determine Heirship Due To Violation Of No Contest Clause By Howard Schuman And For Interpretation Of Trust Provisions Does Not Violate The Terms Of The No Contest Clause
Off Calendar Mar-24-2010 Continued To Apr-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Douglas Applegate for Rita Schuman (Objector/Trustee). Jerome Marks for Stacey Kerreos.

Mr. Marks stated that he received the objections yesterday late afternoon, requested a continuance to respond, and stated that he needed to deal with a clerical error with regard to the date of death for Bessie and Howard. In addition he stated that there was a misstatement of participation as alleged as to Rita Schuman's Children with regard to recording of the deeds, that he would need to delete those allegations, suggested a stipulation in writing with regard to the deletion, stated that they have companion cases appointing the Successor Trustee and that there was an elder abuse matter filed by Mr. Koplowitz. Mr. Marks also stated that the Decedent died two weeks prior to the date of law change with regard to statute no longer allowing safe harbor petitions. Mr. Applegate stated that he had no objection to Counsel's proposed changes as long as he was able to review the new petition. The matter was continued to April 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Marks' response is due by April 5, 2010. Mr. Applegate's reply is due by April 12, 2010.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
